```text
1	# attend
2	
3	Local web dashboard for brief-based AI session management across Claude Code + Codex.
4	
5	> session = cache, brief = state. vendor = replaceable backend, vault = substrate.
6	
7	## What it does
8	
9	- Scans your vaults for `brief.md` files (one per task)
10	- Reads Claude Code JSONL transcripts and matches them to briefs via `cwd`
11	- Derives per-brief telemetry: sessions / prompts / actions / dwell / last touch
12	- Classifies each brief into a behavioral pattern (avoidance / stalled / active / healthy / fresh)
13	- Ranks briefs by `memory-alignment ├Ќ pattern ├Ќ explicit blocker`
14	- Serves one localhost page you can poll instead of N IDE tabs
15	
16	## Quick start
17	
18	```powershell
19	cd D:\workspace\projects\attend
20	pip install -r requirements.txt
21	python daemon.py
22	```
23	
24	Open http://localhost:5050.
25	
26	## Brief format
27	
28	Each task gets a `brief.md` at `projects/<name>/brief.md`:
29	
30	```markdown
31	---
32	status: active            # active | deferred | done
33	last_touch: 2026-05-30
34	defer_until:              # condition, not date Рђћ e.g. "уГЅ X тЏътцЇ"
35	---
36	
37	## what
38	СИђтЈЦУ»Ю, тіа why (СИ║С╗ђС╣ѕУ┐ЎС║Ітђ╝тЙЌтЂџ).
39	
40	## accept
41	done уџётѕцТЇ« (СИЇТў» todo list).
42	
43	## next
44	уј░тюетЇАтюетЊф / СИІСИђТГЦУ»ЦУ»ЋС╗ђС╣ѕ.
45	Т»ЈТгАуд╗т╝ђ session тЅЇТЏ┤Тќ░У┐ЎСИђУАї.
46	```
47	
48	Only `what` / `accept` / `next` sections are read. Other sections are fine Рђћ they're ignored.
49	
50	## Config
51	
52	Edit `CONFIG` at the top of `daemon.py`:
53	
54	- `vault_roots` Рђћ paths scanned recursively for `brief.md`
55	- `claude_projects` Рђћ `~/.claude/projects` (default fits Claude Code)
56	- `codex_sessions` Рђћ `~/.codex/sessions` (optional, currently not parsed in v0)
57	- `memory_file` Рђћ your Claude Code MEMORY.md for keyword extraction
58	- `port` Рђћ default 5050
59	
60	## Pattern definitions
61	
62	| Pattern | Trigger | Meaning |
63	|---|---|---|
64	| `fresh` | 0 sessions | Brief exists, no work started |
65	| `avoidance` | РЅЦ5 prompts, 0 actions | Many entries without productive output Рђћ possible decision avoidance |
66	| `stalled` | 0 actions, last touch РЅЦ 7 days | Cold, no recent activity |
67	| `healthy` | actions > 0, avg dwell РЅЦ 10 min, touch РЅц 3 days | In flow, don't interrupt |
68	| `active` | some actions, otherwise unclassified | Generic in-progress |
69	
70	Telemetry is **descriptive, never judgmental** (Steel 2007 on procrastination self-report Рєњ avoidance feedback loops). All pattern labels are observations, not verdicts.
71	
72	## Priority scoring (v0 heuristic)
73	
74	`score = (memory alignment ├Ќ 2) + pattern weight + explicit blocker bonus Рѕњ defer/done penalty`
75	
76	Each brief gets a one-line reason explaining the rank. Reasons compose so you can override.
77	
78	## Spawn commands
79	
80	Detail page generates copy-paste commands for both Claude and Codex.
81	**v0 does not auto-spawn** Рђћ it copies to clipboard, you paste to your terminal.
82	This is intentional: auto-spawn from dashboard Рєњ 4-tab cascade.
83	
84	## What this is NOT (deliberately)
85	
86	- Not a chat UI Рђћ sessions stay in Claude Code / Codex
87	- Not push notifications Рђћ pull, manual refresh
88	- Not a TODO app Рђћ briefs are task-shaped, not item-shaped
89	- Not multi-user Рђћ single-person attention router
90	- Not Obsidian / Zettelkasten Рђћ flat folder scan, no bidirectional links
91	
92	## v0 Рєњ v1 roadmap
93	
94	- [ ] Codex JSONL telemetry (when schema known)
95	- [ ] Split CLI: `attend split <jsonl-path>` Рєњ N candidate briefs
96	- [ ] Optional LLM-based priority (env-flag, with reasons)
97	- [ ] `attend new <name>` to scaffold brief.md
98	- [ ] Activity heatmap per brief (dwell over time)
99	- [ ] Brief edit in-browser (currently file-system only)
100	
101	## Design invariants
102	
103	Touch these only with cause:
104	
105	1. **brief = state, session = cache** Рђћ nothing the dashboard knows lives only in a session
106	2. **pull, not push** Рђћ no notifications unless user opts in later
107	3. **descriptive telemetry, never judgmental** Рђћ Steel constraint
108	4. **vendor-neutral data, vendor-locked execution** Рђћ JSONL is read; spawn is per-vendor
109	5. **single polling surface** Рђћ adding a second dashboard is a regression
110
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# attend Рђћ design
2	
3	This document captures the design context that led to v0. Subsequent sessions opened in this project should read this first to onboard.
4	
5	## Problem
6	
7	A single person collaborating with AI faces four compounding costs:
8	
9	1. **Concurrency** Рђћ 10+ active sessions across Claude Code (multiple VS Code projects) + Codex CLI is normal, not pathological. Reducing the count is not the goal.
10	2. **Vendor isolation** Рђћ Claude Code and Codex are separate ecosystems. `/fork` exists in each (Codex CLI has it, Claude Code CLI has it but the VS Code panel does not). Neither is cross-vendor. Manual copy-paste is the current state.
11	3. **Decision avoidance** Рђћ when a session needs a hard decision, the user switches to easier completed sessions to "verify and push." Classic Steel-type *avoidant procrastination*. Self-report doesn't detect it (gets rationalized as "efficient batching"). Behavioral telemetry would.
12	4. **Polling overhead** Рђћ N IDE tabs ├Ќ re-orientation cost on each switch (UC Irvine: 23 min 15 s to refocus; APA: 40% productivity loss from switching). Adding more organization tools makes this worse, not better.
13	
14	## Conceptual model Рђћ the invariant
15	
16	```
17	session = cache       (transient, vendor-bound, may die)
18	brief   = state       (persistent, vendor-neutral, survives session death)
19	vendor  = replaceable backend
20	vault   = substrate
21	```
22	
23	A task may use 0..N sessions over its life. Sessions are vehicles; briefs are passengers. **A cold 3-day-old session is usually more expensive to resume than spawning a fresh session from a brief** Рђћ chat history accumulates dead context, summarization loses fidelity, and the user's own working memory of the session has decayed.
24	
25	Concrete consequence: don't try to keep sessions alive across attention gaps. Externalize state to brief; kill the session; spawn fresh when ready.
26	
27	## Four components
28	
29	### 1. Brief
30	
31	- **Granularity**: per-task, not per-session
32	- **Location**: `projects/<name>/brief.md` Рђћ physically colocated with work, not in a separate "briefs/" directory. Found by `ls`, not by remembering.
33	- **Three sections**:
34	  - `what` Рђћ one sentence + *why* this is worth doing
35	  - `accept` Рђћ done criteria (not a TODO list)
36	  - `next` Рђћ **where you are stuck / what to try next**. Update this line before every session-leave. Gollwitzer's implementation-intention research: the meta-decision ("what should I do?") is where cost concentrates; pre-committing it at cheap time pays off massively.
37	- **Front matter**: `status` (active/deferred/done), `defer_until` (a *condition*, not a date Рђћ "уГЅ X тЏътцЇ"), `last_touch`
38	- **Lifecycle**: created manually or via split; killed only by explicit `status: done`. Cold sessions are killed; their briefs persist.
39	
40	### 2. Telemetry
41	
42	- **Data sources**: vendor JSONL transcripts
43	  - Claude Code: `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/*.jsonl`
44	  - Codex CLI: `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/*.jsonl` (not parsed in v0)
45	- **Session-to-brief matching**: by `cwd` field in JSONL. A session's cwd contains (or equals) the brief's project directory.
46	- **Per-brief signals**: sessions count, prompts count, action count (Edit/Write/Bash tool uses), dwell distribution, last_touch_age, last_action_age
47	- **Pattern classifier**:
48	
49	| Pattern | Trigger | What it means |
50	|---|---|---|
51	| `fresh` | 0 sessions | Brief exists, no work started |
52	| `avoidance` | РЅЦ5 prompts, 0 actions | Many entries, no productive output Рђћ decision is what's needed, not more "work" |
53	| `stalled` | 0 actions, last touch РЅЦ 7 days | Cold Рђћ needs unblock or kill |
54	| `healthy` | actions > 0, avg dwell РЅЦ 10 min, touch РЅц 3 days | In flow, don't interrupt |
55	| `active` | actions > 0, otherwise | Generic in-progress |
56	
57	- **Hard constraint** Рђћ descriptive, never judgmental (Steel 2007: judgmental feedback on procrastination behavior *worsens* it via self-esteem mediation). Output strings must avoid second-person pressure verbs ("you again," "still stuck"). Use observation-form: "X count, Y duration, Z actions."
58	
59	### 3. Priority evaluator
60	
61	- **Inputs**: brief ├Ќ user-memory keywords (extracted from MEMORY.md) ├Ќ telemetry pattern ├Ќ explicit blockers in `next`
62	- **Output**: ranked list + **one-line reason per rank** (no opaque scores; user must be able to override)
63	- **Time NOT used as input** Рђћ explicitly rejected (see below). Semantic alignment with memory is the real axis; pattern signals action implication.
64	- v0: heuristic scoring (memory keyword hits ├Ќ 2 + pattern bonus + blocker bonus Рѕњ defer penalty)
65	- v1+: LLM-based, env-flagged (`ATTEND_LLM=anthropic|openai`)
66	
67	### 4. Split (not in v0)
68	
69	- **Purpose**: when a session organically grows N follow-up threads, factor them out into N new briefs
70	- **Flow**: read transcript Рєњ LLM "identify open follow-up threads" Рєњ N candidate briefs in 3-line format Рєњ user reviews Рєњ commits selected to vault
71	- **Intentional limitation**: does NOT auto-spawn sessions. spawn is a separate user decision. Auto-spawn from split would create a 4-tab cascade Рђћ exactly the load problem we're solving.
72	- **Distinction from fork**: fork = duplicate-and-diverge (Warp, Claude SDK, Codex CLI); split = factor-out. Fork copies state; split extracts task specs. They're different operations.
73	- **Cross-vendor**: split reads JSONL on disk Рђћ vendor-neutral input Рєњ vendor-neutral brief output. Vendor only re-enters at spawn time.
74	
75	## Product form
76	
77	**Local web app at `http://localhost:5050`. Python + Flask + server-side HTML.** Daemon scans vault, reads vendor JSONL, renders ranked feed. Polled by browser refresh.
78	
79	### Why this form
80	
81	- **Single polling surface** Рђћ one tab, one URL. Replaces N IDE tabs.
82	- **Vendor-neutral** Рђћ not bound to VS Code or any terminal. Treats Claude and Codex equally.
83	- **Data in local vault** Рђћ daemon is view-only; if it dies the vault is still authoritative.
84	- **Incremental enrichment** Рђћ start static HTML, add buttons (spawn, defer, split) as needed.
85	
86	### Forms rejected
87	
88	- **VS Code plugin** Рђћ locks one IDE; can't span Claude + Codex
89	- **TUI** Рђћ multi-window management collapses back to the tab-bounce problem
90	- **Desktop app** Рђћ engineering overhead without benefit
91	- **Push notifications** Рђћ user confirmed: when polling = idle moments, push doesn't help (can't act when not idle). Pull-only.
92	
93	### Inspirational reference
94	
95	Slock.ai's structural primitives Рђћ channel/thread (persistent, task-shaped) vs. message stream (transient) Рђћ directly informed brief/session separation. We use the structural insight without copying the multi-agent collaboration features.
96	
97	## Design invariants
98	
99	Touch these only with explicit cause:
100	
101	1. **brief = state, session = cache** Рђћ nothing the dashboard knows lives only in a session
102	2. **pull, not push** Рђћ no notifications unless user opts in
103	3. **descriptive telemetry, never judgmental** Рђћ Steel constraint, see telemetry section
104	4. **vendor-neutral data, vendor-locked execution** Рђћ JSONL is read; spawn is per-vendor
105	5. **single polling surface** Рђћ adding a second dashboard is a regression
106	
107	## Rejected paths (don't redo)
108	
109	- **4-bucket organization** (TODO / У┐ЏУАїСИГ / ти▓т«їТѕљ / жЋ┐ТюЪС┐ЮуЋЎ in separate locations). Asks user to remember N locations. Replaced by brief sidecar at task location, found by `ls`.
110	- **Obsidian bidirectional links / Zettelkasten-style PKM**. Explicitly deferred Рђћ user judgment was that base models + memory + agent platforms will deliver this upstream over time. Don't preempt; revisit if upstream stalls.
111	- **Hot/warm/cold by time**. Session state changes fast but total count stays high; recency doesn't track priority. Replaced by memory-aligned semantic priority + pattern-based action implication.
112	- **LangChain Agent Inbox / push notifications**. User's diagnostic cost when polling is already near-zero; push adds noise without enabling action.
113	- **Cap to 2-5 concurrent sessions**. Open РЅа active. Goal isn't reducing tab count, it's reducing re-warming cost via durable briefs.
114	- **Fork as cross-vendor primitive**. Each vendor's fork is locked to its transcript format and API. Brief is the cross-vendor abstraction; fork lives one level below.
115	- **Auto-spawn from dashboard**. Auto-spawning new sessions from priority list would recreate the cascade we just dismantled. Spawn buttons copy commands only.
116	
117	## Cross-vendor data layer
118	
119	Both vendors store transcripts as local JSONL with a `cwd` field. Daemon reads both, matches sessions to briefs by cwd-vs-project-dir containment, derives signals per brief, renders vendor-neutral output. Vendor only re-appears at:
120	
121	1. **Transcript ingestion** (per-vendor JSONL parser)
122	2. **Spawn command** (per-vendor CLI invocation)
123	
124	Everything in between (briefs, dashboard, priority, telemetry) is vendor-agnostic markdown/HTML.
125	
126	## Research touchstones
127	
128	- **Steel, P. (2007)** Рђћ *Arousal, avoidant, and decisional procrastinators: do they exist?* Three procrastination types; foundational for the avoidance pattern definition. Identified behavioral-measurement gap: most procrastination research is self-report, telemetry is largely unexplored. This is the niche the telemetry component fills.
129	- **Gollwitzer, P. (1999)** Рђћ *Implementation intentions.* Pre-committing "if X then Y" at cheap time reduces decision-point friction. Direct rationale for the `next` line discipline.
130	- **Rosenbaum (2014)** Рђћ *Pre-crastination.* Doing the easy thing first to free working memory is a real mechanism, not always avoidance. Distinguishes by whether the easy task ends in 1-2 hours; if longer, it's likely the avoidance pattern.
131	- **Karpathy LLM Wiki pattern** Рђћ `wiki/hot.md` of ~500 words auto-loaded at session start. Brief is our hot-cache analog.
132	- **Slock.ai** Рђћ Persistent channels with transient message streams. Structural inspiration for brief/session split.
133	
134	## v0 Рєњ v1 roadmap
135	
136	- [ ] Codex JSONL parser (need a sample of Codex's schema; user has it via CLI but not yet active)
137	- [ ] `attend split <jsonl-path>` CLI: LLM extracts open follow-up threads Рєњ N candidate briefs to review
138	- [ ] `attend new <project>` to scaffold `projects/<project>/brief.md`
139	- [ ] LLM-based priority option (env-flag)
140	- [ ] Refined blocker regex (only `уГЅ[тЁиСйЊтєЁт«╣]`, not bare `уГЅ` char Рђћ currently triggers false positives on parentheticals like "(СйютЊЂB уГЅ)")
141	- [ ] Dwell distribution heatmap per brief
142	- [ ] In-browser brief edit (currently file-system only)
143	
144	## Out of scope (likely permanently)
145	
146	- Multi-user collaboration (Slock-style server/channel/DM)
147	- Real-time push / SSE / websocket
148	- Chat UI in the dashboard (sessions live in Claude Code / Codex)
149	- Hierarchical task trees (briefs are flat; relations live in the `next` line as references)
150	- Cloud sync (vault is local; OneDrive sync of the vault is incidental, not a feature)
151	
152	## How a future session onboards
153	
154	If you (Claude / Codex / human) are picking this up cold:
155	
156	1. Read this document end-to-end.
157	2. Read `README.md` for run instructions.
158	3. Read `brief.md` for current `next`.
159	4. Open http://localhost:5050 if the daemon is running (`python daemon.py`).
160	5. Pick from the brief's `next` and start.
161	
162	Do not add organizational layers unless you've checked them against invariant 5. Do not change telemetry output to be more "motivating" Рђћ that's invariant 3 violation; see Steel research.
163
```
</details></tool-use>

15	# ----- config -----
16	
17	HOME = Path.home()
18	
19	CONFIG = {

22	        Path("D:/workspace/projects"),
23	    ],
24	    "claude_projects": HOME / ".claude" / "projects",
25	    "codex_sessions": HOME / ".codex" / "sessions",

33	def parse_brief(path):
34	    try:
35	        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
36	    except OSError:
37	        return None
38	    fm = {}
39	    body = text
40	    if text.startswith("---"):
41	        end = text.find("\n---", 3)
42	        if end > 0:
43	            try:
44	                fm = yaml.safe_load(text[3:end]) or {}
45	            except yaml.YAMLError:
46	                fm = {}
47	            body = text[end + 4:].lstrip()
48	    sections = {}
49	    current = None
50	    buf = []
51	    for line in body.splitlines():
52	        m = re.match(r"^#+\s+(\S+)", line)
53	        if m:
54	            if current is not None:
55	                sections[current] = "\n".join(buf).strip()
56	            current = m.group(1).lower()
57	            buf = []
58	        else:
59	            buf.append(line)
60	    if current is not None:
61	        sections[current] = "\n".join(buf).strip()
62	    project_dir = path.parent
63	    return {
64	        "path": str(path),
65	        "project_dir": str(project_dir),
66	        "name": project_dir.name,
67	        "front_matter": fm,
68	        "what": sections.get("what", ""),
69	        "accept": sections.get("accept", ""),
70	        "next": sections.get("next", ""),
71	        "status": (fm.get("status") or "active"),
72	        "defer_until": fm.get("defer_until"),
73	    }
74	
75	def scan_vault():
76	    briefs = []
77	    seen = set()
78	    for root in CONFIG["vault_roots"]:
79	        if not root.exists():
80	            continue
81	        for path in root.rglob("brief.md"):
82	            sp = str(path).lower()
83	            if sp in seen:
84	                continue
85	            seen.add(sp)
86	            b = parse_brief(path)
87	            if b:
88	                briefs.append(b)
89	    return briefs
90	
91	# ----- claude telemetry -----
92	
93	def _parse_ts(s):
94	    if not s:
95	        return None
96	    try:
97	        return datetime.fromisoformat(s.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
98	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
99	        return None
100	
101	def _scan_claude_sessions():
102	    """Yield (jsonl_path, cwd, first_ts, last_ts, prompt_count, action_count)."""
103	    root = CONFIG["claude_projects"]
104	    if not root.exists():
105	        return
106	    for proj_dir in root.iterdir():
107	        if not proj_dir.is_dir():
108	            continue
109	        for jsonl in proj_dir.glob("*.jsonl"):
110	            cwd = None
111	            first_ts = None
112	            last_ts = None
113	            prompts = 0
114	            actions = 0
115	            try:
116	                with jsonl.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
117	                    for line in f:
118	                        try:
119	                            obj = json.loads(line)
120	                        except json.JSONDecodeError:
121	                            continue
122	                        if cwd is None and obj.get("cwd"):
123	                            cwd = obj["cwd"]
124	                        ts = _parse_ts(obj.get("timestamp"))
125	                        if ts:
126	                            if first_ts is None:
127	                                first_ts = ts
128	                            last_ts = ts
129	                        t = obj.get("type")
130	                        if t == "user":
131	                            msg = obj.get("message") or {}
132	                            content = msg.get("content") if isinstance(msg, dict) else None
133	                            if isinstance(content, str) and content.strip() and not content.startswith("<"):
134	                                prompts += 1
135	                            elif isinstance(content, list):
136	                                for c in content:
137	                                    if isinstance(c, dict) and c.get("type") == "text":
138	                                        prompts += 1
139	                                        break
140	                        elif t == "assistant":
141	                            msg = obj.get("message") or {}
142	                            if isinstance(msg, dict):
143	                                for c in msg.get("content", []) or []:
144	                                    if isinstance(c, dict) and c.get("type") == "tool_use":
145	                                        name = c.get("name", "")
146	                                        if name in ("Edit", "Write", "NotebookEdit", "Bash", "PowerShell"):
147	                                            actions += 1
148	            except OSError:
149	                continue
150	            yield {
151	                "path": str(jsonl),
152	                "cwd": cwd,
153	                "first_ts": first_ts,
154	                "last_ts": last_ts,
155	                "prompts": prompts,
156	                "actions": actions,
157	            }
158	
159	_SESSION_CACHE = {"ts": None, "data": []}
160	
161	def get_claude_sessions():
162	    """Cheap in-process cache to avoid re-scanning JSONL on every refresh."""
163	    now = datetime.now()
164	    if _SESSION_CACHE["ts"] and (now - _SESSION_CACHE["ts"]).total_seconds() < 30:
165	        return _SESSION_CACHE["data"]
166	    data = list(_scan_claude_sessions())
167	    _SESSION_CACHE["ts"] = now
168	    _SESSION_CACHE["data"] = data
169	    return data
170	
171	def telemetry_for_brief(brief, sessions):
172	    """Aggregate sessions whose cwd contains or equals the brief's project_dir."""
173	    proj = Path(brief["project_dir"]).resolve()
174	    matched = []
175	    for s in sessions:
176	        if not s["cwd"]:
177	            continue
178	        try:
179	            cwd = Path(s["cwd"]).resolve()
180	        except OSError:
181	            continue
182	        try:
183	            proj.relative_to(cwd)
184	            matched.append(s)
185	            continue
186	        except ValueError:
187	            pass
188	        try:
189	            cwd.relative_to(proj)
190	            matched.append(s)
191	        except ValueError:
192	            pass
193	    if not matched:
194	        return {
195	            "sessions": 0,
196	            "prompts": 0,
197	            "actions": 0,
198	            "total_minutes": 0,
199	            "avg_session_min": None,
200	            "last_action_age_days": None,
201	            "last_touch": None,
202	            "last_touch_age_days": None,
203	        }
204	    total_min = 0.0
205	    durations = []
206	    last_action_ts = None
207	    last_touch_ts = None
208	    for s in matched:
209	        if s["first_ts"] and s["last_ts"]:
210	            d = (s["last_ts"] - s["first_ts"]).total_seconds() / 60
211	            total_min += d
212	            durations.append(d)
213	        if s["last_ts"] and (last_touch_ts is None or s["last_ts"] > last_touch_ts):
214	            last_touch_ts = s["last_ts"]
215	        if s["actions"] > 0 and s["last_ts"]:
216	            if last_action_ts is None or s["last_ts"] > last_action_ts:
217	                last_action_ts = s["last_ts"]
218	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
219	    return {
220	        "sessions": len(matched),
221	        "prompts": sum(s["prompts"] for s in matched),
222	        "actions": sum(s["actions"] for s in matched),
223	        "total_minutes": total_min,
224	        "avg_session_min": (sum(durations) / len(durations)) if durations else None,
225	        "last_action_age_days": ((now - last_action_ts).days if last_action_ts else None),
226	        "last_touch": (last_touch_ts.isoformat() if last_touch_ts else None),
227	        "last_touch_age_days": ((now - last_touch_ts).days if last_touch_ts else None),
228	    }
229	
230	def classify_pattern(tel):
231	    s = tel["sessions"]
232	    a = tel["actions"]
233	    p = tel["prompts"]
234	    avg = tel["avg_session_min"]
235	    age = tel["last_action_age_days"]
236	    touch_age = tel["last_touch_age_days"]
237	    if s == 0:
238	        return "fresh"
239	    if p >= 5 and a == 0:
240	        return "avoidance"
241	    if a == 0 and touch_age is not None and touch_age >= 7:
242	        return "stalled"
243	    if a > 0 and avg and avg >= 10 and touch_age is not None and touch_age <= 3:
244	        return "healthy"
245	    if a > 0:
246	        return "active"
247	    return "unknown"
248	
249	# ----- memory & priority -----
250	
251	_MEMORY_CACHE = {"ts": None, "keywords": []}
252	
253	def load_memory_keywords():
254	    now = datetime.now()
255	    if _MEMORY_CACHE["ts"] and (now - _MEMORY_CACHE["ts"]).total_seconds() < 60:
256	        return _MEMORY_CACHE["keywords"]
257	    memfile = CONFIG["memory_file"]
258	    if not memfile.exists():
259	        return []
260	    try:
261	        text = memfile.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
262	    except OSError:
263	        return []
264	    cjk = re.findall(r"[СИђ-ж┐┐]{2,}", text)
265	    latin = re.findall(r"\b[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]{3,}\b", text)
266	    stop = {"the", "and", "with", "this", "that", "from", "have", "will",
267	            "name", "description", "metadata", "type", "memory", "file"}
268	    keywords = list({w for w in cjk + latin if w.lower() not in stop})
269	    _MEMORY_CACHE["ts"] = now
270	    _MEMORY_CACHE["keywords"] = keywords
271	    return keywords
272	
273	def evaluate_priority(brief, tel, memory_keywords):
274	    blob = " ".join([brief["name"], brief["what"], brief["next"]]).lower()
275	    score = 0.0
276	    reasons = []
277	    kw_hits = sum(1 for k in memory_keywords if k.lower() in blob)
278	    if kw_hits:
279	        bonus = min(kw_hits, 5) * 2
280	        score += bonus
281	        reasons.append(f"memory aligned ({kw_hits})")
282	    pattern = classify_pattern(tel)
283	    if pattern == "avoidance":
284	        score += 4
285	        reasons.append("avoidance signal Рђћ needs decision, not work")
286	    elif pattern == "stalled":
287	        score += 3
288	        reasons.append("stalled Рђћ needs unblock or kill")
289	    elif pattern == "fresh":
290	        score += 1
291	        reasons.append("fresh Рђћ no entries yet")
292	    elif pattern == "active":
293	        score += 0.5
294	    elif pattern == "healthy":
295	        score -= 1
296	        reasons.append("healthy Рђћ in flow, don't interrupt")
297	    if brief["status"] == "deferred":
298	        if brief["defer_until"]:
299	            reasons.append(f"deferred until {brief['defer_until']}")
300	        else:
301	            reasons.append("deferred without condition")
302	        score -= 2
303	    elif brief["status"] == "done":
304	        score = -100.0
305	        reasons.append("done")
306	    nxt = (brief["next"] or "").lower()
307	    if any(k in nxt for k in ["тЇА", "block", "stuck", "уГЅ"]):
308	        score += 1
309	        reasons.append("explicit blocker in next")
310	    return score, "; ".join(reasons) or "no signal", pattern
311	
312	# ----- spawn -----
313	
314	def _quote(s):
315	    return s.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace('"', '\\"')
316	
317	def spawn_command(brief, vendor):
318	    project = brief["project_dir"]
319	    summary = brief["what"].strip()
320	    nxt = brief["next"].strip()
321	    parts = []
322	    if summary:
323	        parts.append(summary)
324	    if nxt:
325	        parts.append(f"\\n\\nNext: {nxt}")
326	    body = _quote("".join(parts))
327	    if vendor == "claude":
328	        return f'cd "{project}"\nclaude "{body}"'
329	    if vendor == "codex":
330	        return f'cd "{project}"\ncodex "{body}"'
331	    return ""
332	
333	# ----- flask -----
334	
335	app = Flask(__name__, template_folder=str(Path(__file__).parent / "templates"))
336	
337	@app.route("/")
338	def feed():
339	    briefs = scan_vault()
340	    sessions = get_claude_sessions()
341	    memory_keywords = load_memory_keywords()
342	    ranked = []
343	    for b in briefs:

346	        ranked.append({"brief": b, "tel": tel, "score": score,

349	    counts = {}
350	    for r in ranked:

352	    return render_template("feed.html", ranked=ranked, counts=counts,
353	                           memory_keywords=len(memory_keywords))
354	
355	@app.route("/brief")

392	                           score=score, reason=reason, sessions=matched[:10],

```md
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Onboarding

`DESIGN.md` is the source of truth for *why* this exists Рђћ read it end-to-end before changing behavior. It covers the problem, the conceptual model, every rejected path (so you don't redo them), and the research touchstones behind the design. `brief.md` holds the current `next` step. `README.md` has the user-facing contract.

## Commands

\```powershell
pip install -r requirements.txt
python daemon.py          # serves http://localhost:5050
\```

There is no test suite, linter, or build step Рђћ this is a single ~400-line Flask script. Verify changes by running the daemon and loading the page.

## Architecture

The entire backend is `daemon.py`; `templates/feed.html` (ranked list) and `templates/detail.html` (per-brief + spawn commands) are the only views. The pipeline, in order, all in `daemon.py`:

1. **`scan_vault()`** Рђћ `rglob("brief.md")` across `CONFIG["vault_roots"]`. Each brief is a markdown file with YAML front matter (`status`, `defer_until`, `last_touch`) plus `## what` / `## accept` / `## next` sections. Only those three sections are parsed; others are ignored. The brief lives *at the task's project dir*, not in a central folder Рђћ `project_dir = path.parent` is the matching key.
2. **`_scan_claude_sessions()`** Рђћ parses Claude Code JSONL transcripts under `~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl`, extracting `cwd`, timestamps, prompt count, and action count (tool_use of `Edit`/`Write`/`NotebookEdit`/`Bash`/`PowerShell`). Cached in-process for 30 s (`_SESSION_CACHE`). Codex JSONL is **not parsed in v0** (schema unknown).
3. **`telemetry_for_brief()`** Рђћ matches sessions to a brief by **cwd containment** in either direction (session cwd inside project dir, or vice versa), then aggregates sessions/prompts/actions/dwell/last-touch.
4. **`classify_pattern()`** Рђћ maps telemetry to `fresh` / `avoidance` / `stalled` / `healthy` / `active`. See the table in `DESIGN.md` / `README.md` for exact thresholds.
5. **`evaluate_priority()`** Рђћ `memory-keyword alignment ├Ќ2 + pattern weight + explicit-blocker bonus Рѕњ defer/done penalty`, returning a score, a composed human-readable reason, and the pattern. Memory keywords come from `CONFIG["memory_file"]` (the user's MEMORY.md), CJK + latin tokens, cached 60 s. **Time is deliberately not a priority input** (see DESIGN.md "Rejected paths").
6. **`spawn_command()`** Рђћ emits copy-paste `cd Рђд && claude/codex "<brief>"`. v0 never auto-spawns; it only renders commands to copy.

Vendor-specific logic is confined to exactly two seams: JSONL ingestion (step 2) and spawn command emission (step 6). Everything between is vendor-neutral.

All paths/ports live in the `CONFIG` dict at the top of `daemon.py` Рђћ there is no separate config file.

## Design invariants (do not violate without explicit cause)

1. **brief = state, session = cache** Рђћ nothing the dashboard knows may live only in a session.
2. **pull, not push** Рђћ no notifications/SSE/websockets.
3. **descriptive telemetry, never judgmental** Рђћ pattern labels and reason strings must be observation-form ("5 prompts, 0 actions"), never second-person pressure ("you again", "still stuck"). This is the Steel 2007 constraint; making output more "motivating" is a regression, not an improvement.
4. **vendor-neutral data, vendor-locked execution** Рђћ keep vendor logic inside the two seams above.
5. **single polling surface** Рђћ adding a second dashboard/tab/tool is a regression. Check new organizational layers against this.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-05-30 13:27:53Z)**_

Created `CLAUDE.md`. Since this codebase already has unusually strong design docs, I kept it lean and oriented around what's *not* obvious from a single file:

- **Onboarding pointer** Рђћ `DESIGN.md` is the real source of truth (especially its "rejected paths" so future sessions don't redo dead ends); CLAUDE.md just routes there rather than duplicating it.
- **Commands** Рђћ `pip install -r requirements.txt` + `python daemon.py`. I noted explicitly that there's no test/lint/build step, so future instances don't go hunting for one.
- **Architecture** Рђћ the 6-stage pipeline through `daemon.py` (scan Рєњ ingest JSONL Рєњ cwd-match Рєњ classify Рєњ score Рєњ spawn), and the key insight that requires reading multiple files: vendor-specific code is confined to exactly two seams (JSONL parsing, spawn emission), everything else is vendor-neutral.
- **Invariants** Рђћ the 5 hard rules, with emphasis on #3 (descriptive-not-judgmental telemetry), since that's a constraint a well-meaning future edit would most easily break.

One thing worth flagging: the `Bash` tool reported this directory as not a git repo (and the environment says so too), but `python daemon.py` and the matching logic point at real paths like `~/.claude/projects` and `D:/workspace/projects`. If you do plan to version this, `.specstory/` is present but there's no `.gitignore` yet.

That's a significant pivot Рђћ Tauri means a native cross-platform app (Rust core + web UI), which is a deliberate reversal of two things you previously wrote into `DESIGN.md` ("desktop app Рђћ engineering overhead without benefit", and localhost-tab as the single polling surface). I'm fine respecting that override since you own the design Рђћ Tauri actually *keeps* the other invariants intact (single surface, pull-not-push, descriptive telemetry, vendor-neutral data), it just changes the shell from a browser tab to a native window and solves cross-platform distribution + native folder-pickers for "ТїЄт«џуЏ«тйЋ" cleanly.

Good catch Рђћ that question exposes that **Tauri is the wrong tool for what you actually want.**

Tauri builds a **native desktop app**: it bundles a Rust binary + the OS WebView into a platform-specific installer (`.msi` / `.dmg` / `.AppImage`). The user *downloads and installs an app*. There's no "open in any browser via localhost" mode Рђћ the UI renders into an embedded window, not a public local server. So Tauri is the opposite of "no download."

What you're describing Рђћ **start a local process, open in the browser, run via `npx` from a remote repo, zero install** Рђћ is the classic **Node CLI that boots a local web server** pattern (`npx serve`, `npx http-server`, `npx vite`). You publish to npm; `npx attend` downloads-and-runs on demand, no permanent install, works on Win/macOS because Node does. This is actually *much closer to your original `DESIGN.md`* (localhost single surface, pull-not-push) than Tauri was Рђћ the only real change from today's Flask app is **Python Рєњ Node/TS**, so that `npx` distribution works.

That said, there's one fork worth locking before I rewrite the plan, since it's the biggest cost driver:

Let me answer your npm/npx/node question directly, then I'll rewrite the plan around it.

## node / npm / npx Рђћ what they are

- **node** Рђћ the JavaScript runtime (think: the Python interpreter, but for JS/TS). Installed once per machine; it's what actually *runs* the local server process. Exists on both Win and macOS.
- **npm** Рђћ Node's package manager. It's two things: РЉа a CLI like `pip`, and РЉА the public registry at `registry.npmjs.org` where packages are published and downloaded.
- **npx** Рђћ a runner bundled with npm that executes a package's command **without permanently installing it**: fetch Рєњ run Рєњ done. The only question is *where* `npx <thing>` resolves the package from.

## Does "npx У┐юуеІ" require publishing to npm? Рђћ No. Three options, cost descending:

1. **Publish to npm registry** Рєњ `npx attend`
   - Cost: free npm account, claim an unused name (or a scope like `@sin/attend`), `npm publish` per release. **Public packages are free** (private ones cost). Cleanest command, anyone can run it.
   - Downsides: code is public; every release is `npm version` + `npm publish`; name-squatting.
2. **npx straight from GitHub** Рєњ `npx github:you/attend` Рђћ **no npm publish at all**
   - Cost: ~zero. Just `git push`. No npm account. Works with private repos (needs a token).
   - Downsides: longer command; needs a build step (`prepare`) or committed `dist/`.
3. **No remote** Рєњ `git clone` once Рєњ `npm install` Рєњ `npm start`
   - You have two of your own machines (Win + Mac); cloning once is trivial. The *only* thing npx-from-remote buys you is running on a brand-new machine without cloning.

**The key insight:** whether you ever `npm publish` does **not** change how I build it. If I make it a standard npm package (a `bin` entry + a `dist/` build), then **all three** work automatically Рђћ you can `npx github:you/attend` today and decide on `npm publish` later, or never. So I'll build for that superset and leave publishing as a final, optional one-liner.

I've rewritten the plan around **Node + TypeScript, npx-distributable local web app** (which also restores your original `DESIGN.md` localhost/single-surface invariant that Tauri would have broken).

As of 2026-05-30, `attend` (D:\workspace\projects\attend) is being rewritten from a single-file Python/Flask `daemon.py` into a **Node + TypeScript** local web app, distributable via `npx` (npm package with a `bin` entry + `dist/` build; supports `npx github:you/attend`, optional `npm publish`, and local `npm start`).

Target architecture: `src/core/` (pure logic, vitest-tested) decoupled from `src/server.ts` (Hono, server-rendered HTML reusing the old Jinja templates' HTML/CSS); `SessionSource` interface as the vendor extension point (claude impl + codex stub). Old `daemon.py` moves to `legacy/`, not deleted.

Design invariants from DESIGN.md still hold: pull-not-push, descriptive-never-judgmental (Steel 2007), vendor-neutral data / vendor-locked execution, single polling surface (localhost). Tauri was explicitly rejected (it's a downloadable native app Рђћ opposite of zero-install). Memory model unchanged Рђћ see [[user-prefers-zero-install-npx]]. /** Shared domain types. session = cache, brief = state. */

export type Pattern = "fresh" | "avoidance" | "stalled" | "healthy" | "active" | "unknown";

export interface BriefFrontMatter {
  status?: string;
  defer_until?: string | null;
  last_touch?: string | null;
  [key: string]: unknown;
}

export interface Brief {
  path: string;
  projectDir: string;
  name: string;
  frontMatter: BriefFrontMatter;
  what: string;
  accept: string;
  next: string;
  /** active | deferred | done */
  status: string;
  deferUntil: string | null;
}

/** One vendor session, normalized across Claude / Codex. */
export interface RawSession {
  path: string;
  vendor: string;
  cwd: string | null;
  /** epoch ms, or null when no timestamps were found */
  firstTs: number | null;
  lastTs: number | null;
  prompts: number;
  actions: number;
}

export interface Telemetry {
  sessions: number;
  prompts: number;
  actions: number;
  totalMinutes: number;
  avgSessionMin: number | null;
  lastActionAgeDays: number | null;
  /** ISO string of most recent touch, or null */
  lastTouch: string | null;
  lastTouchAgeDays: number | null;
}

export interface RankedBrief {
  brief: Brief;
  telemetry: Telemetry;
  score: number;
  reason: string;
  pattern: Pattern;
}

```typescript
import fs from "node:fs";
import path from "node:path";
import type { Brief, BriefFrontMatter } from "./types.js";

/**
 * Minimal flat YAML front-matter parser. The brief format only uses flat
 * `key: value` pairs (status / defer_until / last_touch), so a full YAML
 * dependency is unnecessary. Mirrors daemon.py's `yaml.safe_load` for that shape.
 */
function parseFrontMatter(raw: string): BriefFrontMatter {
  const fm: BriefFrontMatter = {};
  for (const line of raw.split(/\r?\n/)) {
    const m = /^([A-Za-z_][\w-]*)\s*:\s*(.*)$/.exec(line);
    if (!m) continue;
    const key = m[1] as string;
    let value = (m[2] ?? "").trim();
    if (
      (value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"')) ||
      (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'"))
    ) {
      value = value.slice(1, -1);
    }
    fm[key] = value === "" ? null : value;
  }
  return fm;
}

/** Split markdown body into sections keyed by the first token of each heading. */
function parseSections(body: string): Record<string, string> {
  const sections: Record<string, string> = {};
  let current: string | null = null;
  let buf: string[] = [];
  const flush = () => {
    if (current !== null) sections[current] = buf.join("\n").trim();
  };
  for (const line of body.split(/\r?\n/)) {
    const m = /^#+\s+(\S+)/.exec(line);
    if (m) {
      flush();
      current = (m[1] as string).toLowerCase();
      buf = [];
    } else {
      buf.push(line);
    }
  }
  flush();
  return sections;
}

/** Parse a brief.md from its text. Only what/accept/next sections are surfaced. */
export function parseBriefText(filePath: string, text: string): Brief {
  let fm: BriefFrontMatter = {};
  let body = text;
  if (text.startsWith("---")) {
    const end = text.indexOf("\n---", 3);
    if (end > 0) {
      fm = parseFrontMatter(text.slice(3, end));
      body = text.slice(end + 4).replace(/^\s+/, "");
    }
  }
  const sections = parseSections(body);
  const projectDir = path.dirname(filePath);
  const statusRaw = fm.status;
  return {
    path: filePath,
    projectDir,
    name: path.basename(projectDir),
    frontMatter: fm,
    what: sections.what ?? "",
    accept: sections.accept ?? "",
    next: sections.next ?? "",
    status: typeof statusRaw === "string" && statusRaw ? statusRaw : "active",
    deferUntil: (fm.defer_until as string | null | undefined) ?? null,
  };
}

export function parseBrief(filePath: string): Brief | null {
  try {
    return parseBriefText(filePath, fs.readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8"));
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

const IGNORED_DIRS = new Set([
  "node_modules",
  ".git",
  "dist",
  ".specstory",
  ".venv",
  "__pycache__",
  ".next",
  "target",
]);

/** Recursively find files named `filename` under `root`, skipping noisy dirs. */
export function walkFor(root: string, filename: string, maxDepth: number): string[] {
  const found: string[] = [];
  const walk = (dir: string, depth: number) => {
    if (depth > maxDepth) return;
    let entries: fs.Dirent[];
    try {
      entries = fs.readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true });
    } catch {
      return;
    }
    for (const entry of entries) {
      const full = path.join(dir, entry.name);
      if (entry.isDirectory()) {
        if (!IGNORED_DIRS.has(entry.name)) walk(full, depth + 1);
      } else if (entry.isFile() && entry.name === filename) {
        found.push(full);
      }
    }
  };
  walk(root, 0);
  return found;
}

/** Scan every vault root for brief.md files (de-duped, case-insensitive path). */
export function scanVault(vaultRoots: string[], maxDepth = 8): Brief[] {
  const briefs: Brief[] = [];
  const seen = new Set<string>();
  for (const root of vaultRoots) {
    if (!fs.existsSync(root)) continue;
    for (const file of walkFor(root, "brief.md", maxDepth)) {
      const key = file.toLowerCase();
      if (seen.has(key)) continue;
      seen.add(key);
      const b = parseBrief(file);
      if (b) briefs.push(b);
    }
  }
  return briefs;
}

```

```typescript
import type { Pattern, Telemetry } from "./types.js";

/**
 * Map telemetry to a behavioral pattern. Thresholds ported 1:1 from daemon.py
 * `classify_pattern`. Labels are descriptive observations, never verdicts
 * (DESIGN.md invariant 3, Steel 2007).
 */
export function classifyPattern(tel: Telemetry): Pattern {
  const { sessions, actions, prompts, avgSessionMin, lastTouchAgeDays } = tel;
  if (sessions === 0) return "fresh";
  if (prompts >= 5 && actions === 0) return "avoidance";
  if (actions === 0 && lastTouchAgeDays !== null && lastTouchAgeDays >= 7) return "stalled";
  if (
    actions > 0 &&
    avgSessionMin !== null &&
    avgSessionMin >= 10 &&
    lastTouchAgeDays !== null &&
    lastTouchAgeDays <= 3
  ) {
    return "healthy";
  }
  if (actions > 0) return "active";
  return "unknown";
}

```

```typescript
import path from "node:path";
import type { Brief, RawSession, Telemetry } from "./types.js";

const DAY_MS = 86_400_000;
const MIN_MS = 60_000;

/** True if `child` is the same as or nested under `parent`. */
function isInside(parent: string, child: string): boolean {
  const rel = path.relative(parent, child);
  return rel === "" || (!rel.startsWith("..") && !path.isAbsolute(rel));
}

/** Sessions match a brief when its project dir contains, or is contained by, the session cwd. */
export function matchSessions(brief: Brief, sessions: RawSession[]): RawSession[] {
  const proj = path.resolve(brief.projectDir);
  const matched: RawSession[] = [];
  for (const s of sessions) {
    if (!s.cwd) continue;
    const cwd = path.resolve(s.cwd);
    if (isInside(cwd, proj) || isInside(proj, cwd)) matched.push(s);
  }
  return matched;
}

const EMPTY: Telemetry = {
  sessions: 0,
  prompts: 0,
  actions: 0,
  totalMinutes: 0,
  avgSessionMin: null,
  lastActionAgeDays: null,
  lastTouch: null,
  lastTouchAgeDays: null,
};

export function telemetryForBrief(
  brief: Brief,
  sessions: RawSession[],
  now: number = Date.now(),
): Telemetry {
  const matched = matchSessions(brief, sessions);
  if (matched.length === 0) return { ...EMPTY };

  let totalMinutes = 0;
  const durations: number[] = [];
  let lastActionTs: number | null = null;
  let lastTouchTs: number | null = null;
  let prompts = 0;
  let actions = 0;

  for (const s of matched) {
    prompts += s.prompts;
    actions += s.actions;
    if (s.firstTs !== null && s.lastTs !== null) {
      const d = (s.lastTs - s.firstTs) / MIN_MS;
      totalMinutes += d;
      durations.push(d);
    }
    if (s.lastTs !== null && (lastTouchTs === null || s.lastTs > lastTouchTs)) {
      lastTouchTs = s.lastTs;
    }
    if (s.actions > 0 && s.lastTs !== null && (lastActionTs === null || s.lastTs > lastActionTs)) {
      lastActionTs = s.lastTs;
    }
  }

  return {
    sessions: matched.length,
    prompts,
    actions,
    totalMinutes,
    avgSessionMin: durations.length
      ? durations.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / durations.length
      : null,
    lastActionAgeDays: lastActionTs !== null ? Math.floor((now - lastActionTs) / DAY_MS) : null,
    lastTouch: lastTouchTs !== null ? new Date(lastTouchTs).toISOString() : null,
    lastTouchAgeDays: lastTouchTs !== null ? Math.floor((now - lastTouchTs) / DAY_MS) : null,
  };
}

```typescript
import fs from "node:fs";
import path from "node:path";

const STOPWORDS = new Set([
  "the",
  "and",
  "with",
  "this",
  "that",
  "from",
  "have",
  "will",
  "name",
  "description",
  "metadata",
  "type",
  "memory",
  "file",
]);

const CJK = /[СИђ-ж┐┐]{2,}/g;
const LATIN = /\b[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]{3,}\b/g;

/**
 * Discover Claude Code per-project memory files. Memory model is unchanged from
 * Claude: each project keeps its own `memory/MEMORY.md` under
 * ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/. We union them as the user's memory corpus.
 */
export function discoverMemorySources(claudeProjects: string): string[] {
  const sources: string[] = [];
  let entries: fs.Dirent[];
  try {
    entries = fs.readdirSync(claudeProjects, { withFileTypes: true });
  } catch {
    return sources;
  }
  for (const entry of entries) {
    if (!entry.isDirectory()) continue;
    const mem = path.join(claudeProjects, entry.name, "memory", "MEMORY.md");
    if (fs.existsSync(mem)) sources.push(mem);
  }
  return sources;
}

/** Extract a deduped keyword set (CJK bigrams+ and latin words) from memory files. */
export function loadMemoryKeywords(sources: string[]): string[] {
  const keywords = new Set<string>();
  for (const src of sources) {
    let text: string;
    try {
      text = fs.readFileSync(src, "utf-8");
    } catch {
      continue;
    }
    for (const w of text.match(CJK) ?? []) keywords.add(w);
    for (const w of text.match(LATIN) ?? []) {
      if (!STOPWORDS.has(w.toLowerCase())) keywords.add(w);
    }
  }
  return [...keywords];
}

```

```typescript
import { classifyPattern } from "./pattern.js";
import type { Brief, Pattern, Telemetry } from "./types.js";

export interface PriorityResult {
  score: number;
  reason: string;
  pattern: Pattern;
}

/** Blocker present in `next`. Refines daemon.py's bare-"уГЅ" check so that a
 *  trailing "уГЅ" inside parentheticals (e.g. "(СйютЊЂB уГЅ)") no longer false-fires;
 *  "уГЅ" only counts when followed by concrete content. */
function hasBlocker(next: string): boolean {
  const lower = next.toLowerCase();
  if (lower.includes("block") || lower.includes("stuck") || next.includes("тЇА")) return true;
  return /уГЅ[^\s)№╝Ѕ.сђѓ,№╝ї]/.test(next);
}

/**
 * Heuristic priority: memory alignment ├Ќ2 + pattern weight + blocker bonus
 * Рѕњ defer/done penalty. Returns a composed, human-readable reason so the user
 * can override (DESIGN.md: no opaque scores). Ported from daemon.py.
 */
export function evaluatePriority(
  brief: Brief,
  tel: Telemetry,
  memoryKeywords: string[],
): PriorityResult {
  const blob = `${brief.name} ${brief.what} ${brief.next}`.toLowerCase();
  let score = 0;
  const reasons: string[] = [];

  const kwHits = memoryKeywords.reduce((n, k) => (blob.includes(k.toLowerCase()) ? n + 1 : n), 0);
  if (kwHits > 0) {
    score += Math.min(kwHits, 5) * 2;
    reasons.push(`memory aligned (${kwHits})`);
  }

  const pattern = classifyPattern(tel);
  if (pattern === "avoidance") {
    score += 4;
    reasons.push("avoidance signal Рђћ needs decision, not work");
  } else if (pattern === "stalled") {
    score += 3;
    reasons.push("stalled Рђћ needs unblock or kill");
  } else if (pattern === "fresh") {
    score += 1;
    reasons.push("fresh Рђћ no entries yet");
  } else if (pattern === "active") {
    score += 0.5;
  } else if (pattern === "healthy") {
    score -= 1;
    reasons.push("healthy Рђћ in flow, don't interrupt");
  }

  if (brief.status === "deferred") {
    reasons.push(brief.deferUntil ? `deferred until ${brief.deferUntil}` : "deferred without condition");
    score -= 2;
  } else if (brief.status === "done") {
    score = -100;
    reasons.push("done");
  }

  if (hasBlocker(brief.next)) {
    score += 1;
    reasons.push("explicit blocker in next");
  }

  return { score, reason: reasons.join("; ") || "no signal", pattern };
}

```

import type { Brief } from "./types.js";

export type Vendor = "claude" | "codex";

function quote(s: string): string {
  return s.replace(/\\/g, "\\\\").replace(/"/g, '\\"');
}

/**
 * Build a copy-paste spawn command. v0 never auto-spawns Рђћ the dashboard only
 * renders the command for the user to paste (DESIGN.md: avoid the 4-tab cascade).
 */
export function spawnCommand(brief: Brief, vendor: Vendor): string {
  const summary = brief.what.trim();
  const next = brief.next.trim();
  const parts: string[] = [];
  if (summary) parts.push(summary);
  if (next) parts.push(`\\n\\nNext: ${next}`);
  const body = quote(parts.join(""));
  return `cd "${brief.projectDir}"\n${vendor} "${body}"`;
}

import type { RawSession } from "../types.js";
import { ClaudeSource } from "./claude.js";
import { CodexSource } from "./codex.js";

/**
 * A vendor transcript backend. New vendors = new implementation; everything
 * downstream (telemetry, priority, UI) is vendor-neutral (DESIGN.md invariant 4).
 */
export interface SessionSource {
  readonly vendor: string;
  scan(): RawSession[];
}

export interface SessionSourceConfig {
  claudeProjects: string;
  codexSessions: string;
}

export function buildSources(config: SessionSourceConfig): SessionSource[] {
  return [new ClaudeSource(config.claudeProjects), new CodexSource(config.codexSessions)];
}

/** Collect sessions from every vendor source. */
export function collectSessions(config: SessionSourceConfig): RawSession[] {
  return buildSources(config).flatMap((s) => s.scan());
}

export { CodexSource } from "./codex.js";

```typescript
import fs from "node:fs";
import path from "node:path";
import type { RawSession } from "../types.js";
import type { SessionSource } from "./index.js";

/** Tool uses that count as productive "actions". */
const ACTION_TOOLS = new Set(["Edit", "Write", "NotebookEdit", "Bash", "PowerShell"]);

function parseTs(value: unknown): number | null {
  if (typeof value !== "string") return null;
  const t = Date.parse(value);
  return Number.isNaN(t) ? null : t;
}

interface JsonlEntry {
  cwd?: string;
  timestamp?: string;
  type?: string;
  message?: { content?: unknown };
}

function isTextPrompt(content: unknown): boolean {
  if (typeof content === "string") {
    return content.trim() !== "" && !content.startsWith("<");
  }
  if (Array.isArray(content)) {
    return content.some((c) => c && typeof c === "object" && (c as { type?: string }).type === "text");
  }
  return false;
}

function countActions(content: unknown): number {
  if (!Array.isArray(content)) return 0;
  let n = 0;
  for (const c of content) {
    if (c && typeof c === "object") {
      const block = c as { type?: string; name?: string };
      if (block.type === "tool_use" && block.name && ACTION_TOOLS.has(block.name)) n += 1;
    }
  }
  return n;
}

function parseSessionFile(file: string): RawSession {
  const session: RawSession = {
    path: file,
    vendor: "claude",
    cwd: null,
    firstTs: null,
    lastTs: null,
    prompts: 0,
    actions: 0,
  };
  let raw: string;
  try {
    raw = fs.readFileSync(file, "utf-8");
  } catch {
    return session;
  }
  for (const line of raw.split(/\r?\n/)) {
    if (!line.trim()) continue;
    let obj: JsonlEntry;
    try {
      obj = JSON.parse(line) as JsonlEntry;
    } catch {
      continue;
    }
    if (session.cwd === null && obj.cwd) session.cwd = obj.cwd;
    const ts = parseTs(obj.timestamp);
    if (ts !== null) {
      if (session.firstTs === null) session.firstTs = ts;
      session.lastTs = ts;
    }
    if (obj.type === "user") {
      if (isTextPrompt(obj.message?.content)) session.prompts += 1;
    } else if (obj.type === "assistant") {
      session.actions += countActions(obj.message?.content);
    }
  }
  return session;
}

/** Reads Claude Code transcripts at ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/*.jsonl. */
export class ClaudeSource implements SessionSource {
  readonly vendor = "claude";

  constructor(private readonly projectsDir: string) {}

  scan(): RawSession[] {
    const sessions: RawSession[] = [];
    let projects: fs.Dirent[];
    try {
      projects = fs.readdirSync(this.projectsDir, { withFileTypes: true });
    } catch {
      return sessions;
    }
    for (const proj of projects) {
      if (!proj.isDirectory()) continue;
      const dir = path.join(this.projectsDir, proj.name);
      let files: string[];
      try {
        files = fs.readdirSync(dir);
      } catch {
        continue;
      }
      for (const f of files) {
        if (f.endsWith(".jsonl")) sessions.push(parseSessionFile(path.join(dir, f)));
      }
    }
    return sessions;
  }
}

```

```typescript
import fs from "node:fs";
import os from "node:os";
import path from "node:path";

export interface AttendConfig {
  /** Directories scanned recursively for brief.md. */
  vaultRoots: string[];
  /** ~/.claude/projects */
  claudeProjects: string;
  /** ~/.codex/sessions */
  codexSessions: string;
  /** Explicit memory files; when empty, per-project memory is auto-discovered. */
  memorySources: string[];
  port: number;
  host: string;
  /** Open the browser on start. */
  open: boolean;
  scanDepth: number;
}

/** CLI-derived inputs (already parsed by cli.ts). */
export interface CliInputs {
  positionals: string[];
  port?: string;
  host?: string;
  config?: string;
  noOpen?: boolean;
}

interface ConfigFile {
  vaultRoots?: string[];
  claudeProjects?: string;
  codexSessions?: string;
  memorySources?: string[];
  port?: number;
  host?: string;
}

function platformDefaults(): AttendConfig {
  const home = os.homedir();
  return {
    vaultRoots: [process.cwd()],
    claudeProjects: path.join(home, ".claude", "projects"),
    codexSessions: path.join(home, ".codex", "sessions"),
    memorySources: [],
    port: 5050,
    host: "127.0.0.1",
    open: true,
    scanDepth: 8,
  };
}

function readConfigFile(explicitPath: string | undefined): ConfigFile {
  const candidate = explicitPath ?? path.join(process.cwd(), "attend.config.json");
  try {
    if (!fs.existsSync(candidate)) return {};
    return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(candidate, "utf-8")) as ConfigFile;
  } catch {
    return {};
  }
}

function splitPaths(value: string | undefined): string[] | undefined {
  if (!value) return undefined;
  return value
    .split(path.delimiter)
    .map((p) => p.trim())
    .filter(Boolean);
}

/**
 * Resolve config with precedence: CLI args > env > config file > platform defaults.
 * Vault roots / session dirs / port can all be specified, satisfying "тЈ»ТїЄт«џуЏ«тйЋ".
 */
export function resolveConfig(cli: CliInputs): AttendConfig {
  const defaults = platformDefaults();
  const file = readConfigFile(cli.config);
  const env = process.env;

  const vaultRoots =
    cli.positionals.length > 0
      ? cli.positionals
      : (splitPaths(env.ATTEND_VAULTS) ?? file.vaultRoots ?? defaults.vaultRoots);

  const port = Number(cli.port ?? env.ATTEND_PORT ?? file.port ?? defaults.port);

  return {
    vaultRoots: vaultRoots.map((p) => path.resolve(p)),
    claudeProjects: path.resolve(
      env.ATTEND_CLAUDE_PROJECTS ?? file.claudeProjects ?? defaults.claudeProjects,
    ),
    codexSessions: path.resolve(
      env.ATTEND_CODEX_SESSIONS ?? file.codexSessions ?? defaults.codexSessions,
    ),
    memorySources: (file.memorySources ?? defaults.memorySources).map((p) => path.resolve(p)),
    port: Number.isFinite(port) ? port : defaults.port,
    host: cli.host ?? env.ATTEND_HOST ?? file.host ?? defaults.host,
    open: cli.noOpen ? false : defaults.open,
    scanDepth: defaults.scanDepth,
  };
}

```

```typescript
import { evaluatePriority } from "./priority.js";
import { telemetryForBrief } from "./telemetry.js";
import type { Brief, Pattern, RankedBrief, RawSession } from "./types.js";

/** Compute telemetry + priority for each brief and sort by descending score. */
export function rankBriefs(
  briefs: Brief[],
  sessions: RawSession[],
  memoryKeywords: string[],
  now: number = Date.now(),
): RankedBrief[] {
  const ranked = briefs.map((brief) => {
    const telemetry = telemetryForBrief(brief, sessions, now);
    const { score, reason, pattern } = evaluatePriority(brief, telemetry, memoryKeywords);
    return { brief, telemetry, score, reason, pattern };
  });
  ranked.sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);
  return ranked;
}

export function patternCounts(ranked: RankedBrief[]): Record<Pattern, number> {
  const counts = {} as Record<Pattern, number>;
  for (const r of ranked) counts[r.pattern] = (counts[r.pattern] ?? 0) + 1;
  return counts;
}

```

/** HTML-escape arbitrary content (Jinja autoescape equivalent). */
export function esc(value: unknown): string {
  return String(value ?? "")
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;")
    .replace(/'/g, "&#39;");
}

const STYLE = `

  .title a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

`;

const PILL_ORDER: Pattern[] = ["avoidance", "stalled", "active", "fresh", "healthy", "unknown"];

function metaLine(r: RankedBrief): string {
  const t = r.telemetry;
  if (!t.sessions) return `no sessions matched ┬и <span class="reason">${esc(r.reason)}</span>`;
  const bits = [`${t.sessions} sessions ┬и ${t.prompts} prompts ┬и ${t.actions} actions`];
  if (t.avgSessionMin) bits.push(`avg ${Math.round(t.avgSessionMin)}m`);
  if (t.lastTouchAgeDays !== null) bits.push(`touched ${t.lastTouchAgeDays}d ago`);
  return `${bits.join(" ┬и ")} ┬и <span class="reason">${esc(r.reason)}</span>`;
}

function card(r: RankedBrief): string {
  const b = r.brief;
  const doneClass = b.status === "done" ? " done" : "";
  return `
<div class="brief ${r.pattern}${doneClass}">
  <div class="title">
    <a href="/brief?path=${encodeURIComponent(b.path)}">${esc(b.name)}</a>
    <span class="badges">
      <span class="pattern">${esc(r.pattern)}</span>
      score ${r.score.toFixed(1)}
    </span>
  </div>
  ${b.what ? `<div class="what">${esc(b.what)}</div>` : ""}
  ${b.next ? `<div class="next">${esc(b.next)}</div>` : ""}
  <div class="meta">${metaLine(r)}</div>
</div>`;
}

export function renderFeed(
  ranked: RankedBrief[],
  counts: Record<Pattern, number>,
  memoryKeywords: number,
): string {
  const pills = PILL_ORDER.filter((p) => counts[p])
    .map((p) => `<span>${p}: ${counts[p]}</span>`)
    .join("");
  const body = ranked.length
    ? ranked.map(card).join("")
    : `<div class="empty">No <code>brief.md</code> found in any vault root.<br>Create one at <code>projects/&lt;name&gt;/brief.md</code> to start.</div>`;
  return `<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="zh">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8">
<title>attend</title>
<style>${STYLE}</style>
</head>
<body>
<div class="topbar">
  <h1>attend <span class="accent">${ranked.length} briefs ┬и memory keywords: ${memoryKeywords}</span></h1>
  <button onclick="location.reload()">refresh</button>
</div>
<div class="pill-row">${pills}</div>
${body}
</body>
</html>`;
}

```

  }

    background: #1f2937; color: white; border: none; padding: 0.4rem 0.9rem;

export interface DetailView {
  brief: Brief;
  telemetry: Telemetry;
  pattern: Pattern;
  score: number;
  reason: string;
  sessions: RawSession[];
  spawnClaude: string;
  spawnCodex: string;
}

function metric(value: string, key: string): string {
  return `<div class="metric"><div class="v">${value}</div><div class="k">${key}</div></div>`;
}

function baseName(p: string): string {
  const parts = p.split(/[\\/]/);
  return parts[parts.length - 1] ?? p;
}

function sessionRow(s: RawSession): string {
  const when = s.lastTs !== null ? new Date(s.lastTs).toISOString().slice(0, 10) : "";
  return `<div class="session-row">
    <span>${esc(baseName(s.path))}</span>
    <span class="when">${when} ┬и ${s.prompts}p / ${s.actions}a</span>
  </div>`;
}

export function renderDetail(v: DetailView): string {
  const t = v.telemetry;
  const avg = t.avgSessionMin ? `${Math.round(t.avgSessionMin)}m` : "Рђћ";
  const lastAction = t.lastActionAgeDays !== null ? `${t.lastActionAgeDays}d` : "Рђћ";
  const lastTouch = t.lastTouchAgeDays !== null ? `${t.lastTouchAgeDays}d` : "Рђћ";
  const sessionsCard = v.sessions.length
    ? `<div class="card">
  <div class="label">recent sessions (${v.sessions.length})</div>
  ${v.sessions.map(sessionRow).join("")}
</div>`
    : "";

  return `<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="zh">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8">
<title>${esc(v.brief.name)} Рђћ attend</title>
<style>${STYLE}</style>
</head>
<body>
<div class="back"><a href="/">Рєљ all briefs</a></div>
<h1>${esc(v.brief.name)} <span class="pattern ${v.pattern}">${esc(v.pattern)}</span></h1>
<div class="reason">score ${v.score.toFixed(1)} ┬и ${esc(v.reason)}</div>

<div class="card"><div class="label">what</div><div class="body">${esc(v.brief.what)}</div></div>
<div class="card"><div class="label">accept</div><div class="body">${esc(v.brief.accept)}</div></div>
<div class="card"><div class="label">next</div><div class="body">${esc(v.brief.next)}</div></div>

<div class="card">
  <div class="label">telemetry</div>
  <div class="telemetry-grid">
    ${metric(String(t.sessions), "sessions")}
    ${metric(String(t.prompts), "prompts")}
    ${metric(String(t.actions), "actions")}
    ${metric(avg, "avg dwell")}
    ${metric(lastAction, "last action")}
    ${metric(lastTouch, "last touch")}
  </div>
</div>

${sessionsCard}

<div class="card">
  <div class="label">spawn (copy to terminal)</div>
  <div style="margin: 0.5rem 0;">
    <button onclick="copyCmd('claude-cmd')">copy claude</button>
    <button onclick="copyCmd('codex-cmd')">copy codex</button>
  </div>
  <pre id="claude-cmd">${esc(v.spawnClaude)}</pre>
  <pre id="codex-cmd">${esc(v.spawnCodex)}</pre>
</div>

<div class="card"><div class="label">file</div><div class="path">${esc(v.brief.path)}</div></div>

<script>
function copyCmd(id) {
  const text = document.getElementById(id).textContent;
  navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).then(() => {
    document.querySelectorAll("button").forEach(b => {
      if (b.getAttribute("onclick") === "copyCmd('" + id + "')") {
        const orig = b.textContent;
        b.textContent = "copied РюЊ";
        setTimeout(() => { b.textContent = orig; }, 1200);
      }
    });
  });
}
</script>
</body>
</html>`;
}

```typescript
import fs from "node:fs";
import { serve } from "@hono/node-server";
import { Hono } from "hono";
import type { AttendConfig } from "./config.js";
import { parseBrief, scanVault } from "./core/brief.js";
import { discoverMemorySources, loadMemoryKeywords } from "./core/memory.js";
import { evaluatePriority } from "./core/priority.js";
import { patternCounts, rankBriefs } from "./core/rank.js";
import { spawnCommand } from "./core/spawn.js";
import { matchSessions, telemetryForBrief } from "./core/telemetry.js";
import type { RawSession } from "./core/types.js";
import { collectSessions } from "./core/vendor/index.js";
import { renderDetail } from "./ui/detail.js";
import { renderFeed } from "./ui/feed.js";

/** Memoize an expensive scan so browser refreshes don't re-read every JSONL. */
function ttlCache<T>(ttlMs: number, fn: () => T): () => T {
  let stamp = 0;
  let value: T;
  let primed = false;
  return () => {
    const now = Date.now();
    if (primed && now - stamp < ttlMs) return value;
    value = fn();
    stamp = now;
    primed = true;
    return value;
  };
}

export function createApp(config: AttendConfig): Hono {
  const getSessions = ttlCache(30_000, () => collectSessions(config));
  const getKeywords = ttlCache(60_000, () => {
    const sources = config.memorySources.length
      ? config.memorySources
      : discoverMemorySources(config.claudeProjects);
    return loadMemoryKeywords(sources);
  });

  const app = new Hono();

  app.get("/", (c) => {
    const briefs = scanVault(config.vaultRoots, config.scanDepth);
    const ranked = rankBriefs(briefs, getSessions(), getKeywords());
    return c.html(renderFeed(ranked, patternCounts(ranked), getKeywords().length));
  });

  app.get("/brief", (c) => {
    const path = c.req.query("path");
    if (!path) return c.text("missing path", 400);
    if (!fs.existsSync(path)) return c.text("not found", 404);
    const brief = parseBrief(path);
    if (!brief) return c.text("parse failed", 500);

    const sessions = getSessions();
    const telemetry = telemetryForBrief(brief, sessions);
    const { score, reason, pattern } = evaluatePriority(brief, telemetry, getKeywords());
    const matched = matchSessions(brief, sessions)
      .sort((a: RawSession, b: RawSession) => (b.lastTs ?? 0) - (a.lastTs ?? 0))
      .slice(0, 10);

    return c.html(
      renderDetail({
        brief,
        telemetry,
        pattern,
        score,
        reason,
        sessions: matched,
        spawnClaude: spawnCommand(brief, "claude"),
        spawnCodex: spawnCommand(brief, "codex"),
      }),
    );
  });

  return app;
}

export interface RunningServer {
  url: string;
  close: () => void;
}

/** Start the HTTP server; resolves once it is listening. */
export function startServer(config: AttendConfig): Promise<RunningServer> {
  const app = createApp(config);
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    const server = serve({ fetch: app.fetch, hostname: config.host, port: config.port }, () => {
      resolve({
        url: `http://${config.host}:${config.port}`,
        close: () => server.close(),
      });
    });
  });
}

```

```typescript
import { parseArgs } from "node:util";
import open from "open";
import { resolveConfig } from "./config.js";
import { startServer } from "./server.js";

const HELP = `attend Рђћ local web dashboard for brief-based AI session management

Usage:
  attend [dirs...] [options]

Arguments:
  dirs                 Vault roots to scan for brief.md (default: current directory)

Options:
  -p, --port <n>       Port to listen on (default: 5050)
      --host <addr>    Host to bind (default: 127.0.0.1)
  -c, --config <path>  Path to attend.config.json
      --no-open        Do not open the browser on start
  -h, --help           Show this help

Config precedence: CLI args > env (ATTEND_VAULTS / ATTEND_PORT / ATTEND_CLAUDE_PROJECTS
/ ATTEND_CODEX_SESSIONS / ATTEND_HOST) > attend.config.json > platform defaults.`;

async function main(): Promise<void> {
  const { values, positionals } = parseArgs({
    allowPositionals: true,
    options: {
      port: { type: "string", short: "p" },
      host: { type: "string" },
      config: { type: "string", short: "c" },
      "no-open": { type: "boolean" },
      help: { type: "boolean", short: "h" },
    },
  });

  if (values.help) {
    process.stdout.write(`${HELP}\n`);
    return;
  }

  const config = resolveConfig({
    positionals,
    port: values.port,
    host: values.host,
    config: values.config,
    noOpen: values["no-open"],
  });

  const server = await startServer(config);

  process.stdout.write(`attend Рђћ running at ${server.url}\n`);
  process.stdout.write("vault roots:\n");
  for (const root of config.vaultRoots) process.stdout.write(`  ${root}\n`);

  if (config.open) {
    await open(server.url).catch(() => {
      /* headless / no browser Рђћ ignore */
    });
  }
}

main().catch((err) => {
  process.stderr.write(`attend failed to start: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : err}\n`);
  process.exitCode = 1;
});

```

## what
do the thing, why it matters

done criteria here

    expect(b.accept).toBe("");
  });

  it("ignores sections other than what/accept/next", () => {
    const b = parseBriefText("/v/p/brief.md", "## what\nw\n\n## notes\nignored\n");
    expect(b.what).toBe("w");
    expect(b.next).toBe("");
  });
});

import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { matchSessions, telemetryForBrief } from "../src/core/telemetry.js";
import type { Brief, RawSession } from "../src/core/types.js";

const NOW = Date.parse("2026-05-30T00:00:00Z");
const DAY = 86_400_000;

function brief(projectDir: string): Brief {
  return {
    path: `${projectDir}/brief.md`,
    projectDir,
    name: "proj",
    frontMatter: {},
    what: "",
    accept: "",
    next: "",
    status: "active",
    deferUntil: null,
  };
}

function session(cwd: string | null, extra: Partial<RawSession> = {}): RawSession {
  return {
    path: "s.jsonl",
    vendor: "claude",
    cwd,
    firstTs: null,
    lastTs: null,
    prompts: 0,
    actions: 0,
    ...extra,
  };
}

describe("matchSessions (bidirectional cwd containment)", () => {
  const b = brief("D:\\workspace\\proj");

  it("matches when cwd is the project dir, nested under it, or a parent of it", () => {
    const sessions = [
      session("D:\\workspace\\proj"),
      session("D:\\workspace\\proj\\sub"),
      session("D:\\workspace"),
      session("D:\\workspace\\other"),
      session(null),
    ];
    expect(matchSessions(b, sessions)).toHaveLength(3);
  });

  it("normalizes separators so forward-slash cwd matches backslash project dir", () => {
    expect(matchSessions(b, [session("D:/workspace/proj")])).toHaveLength(1);
  });
});

describe("telemetryForBrief", () => {
  it("returns zeros when nothing matches", () => {
    const t = telemetryForBrief(brief("/a/b"), [session("/c/d")], NOW);
    expect(t.sessions).toBe(0);
    expect(t.avgSessionMin).toBeNull();
    expect(t.lastTouchAgeDays).toBeNull();
  });

  it("aggregates prompts/actions/dwell and ages from matched sessions", () => {
    const start = NOW - 2 * DAY;
    const t = telemetryForBrief(brief("/a/b"), [
      session("/a/b", {
        firstTs: start,
        lastTs: start + 30 * 60_000,
        prompts: 4,
        actions: 2,
      }),
      session("/a/b/sub", {
        firstTs: start,
        lastTs: start + 10 * 60_000,
        prompts: 1,
        actions: 0,
      }),
    ], NOW);
    expect(t.sessions).toBe(2);
    expect(t.prompts).toBe(5);
    expect(t.actions).toBe(2);
    expect(t.avgSessionMin).toBe(20);
    expect(t.lastTouchAgeDays).toBe(2);
    expect(t.lastActionAgeDays).toBe(2);
  });
});

import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { classifyPattern } from "../src/core/pattern.js";
import type { Telemetry } from "../src/core/types.js";

function tel(over: Partial<Telemetry>): Telemetry {
  return {
    sessions: 1,
    prompts: 0,
    actions: 0,
    totalMinutes: 0,
    avgSessionMin: null,
    lastActionAgeDays: null,
    lastTouch: null,
    lastTouchAgeDays: null,
    ...over,
  };
}

describe("classifyPattern", () => {
  it("fresh when no sessions", () => {
    expect(classifyPattern(tel({ sessions: 0 }))).toBe("fresh");
  });

  it("avoidance: many prompts, zero actions", () => {
    expect(classifyPattern(tel({ prompts: 5, actions: 0 }))).toBe("avoidance");
  });

  it("stalled: zero actions, cold for >=7 days", () => {
    expect(classifyPattern(tel({ prompts: 2, actions: 0, lastTouchAgeDays: 9 }))).toBe("stalled");
  });

  it("healthy: actions, deep dwell, touched recently", () => {
    expect(
      classifyPattern(tel({ actions: 3, avgSessionMin: 25, lastTouchAgeDays: 1 })),
    ).toBe("healthy");
  });

  it("active: actions present but not healthy", () => {
    expect(classifyPattern(tel({ actions: 1, avgSessionMin: 4, lastTouchAgeDays: 5 }))).toBe(
      "active",
    );
  });
});

import { evaluatePriority } from "../src/core/priority.js";
import type { Brief, Telemetry } from "../src/core/types.js";

function brief(over: Partial<Brief>): Brief {

    projectDir: "/v/p",
    name: "p",
    frontMatter: {},
    what: "",
    accept: "",
    next: "",
    status: "active",
    deferUntil: null,
    ...over,
  };
}

function tel(over: Partial<Telemetry>): Telemetry {
  return {
    sessions: 1,
    prompts: 0,
    actions: 0,
    totalMinutes: 0,
    avgSessionMin: null,
    lastActionAgeDays: null,
    lastTouch: null,
    lastTouchAgeDays: null,
    ...over,
  };
}

describe("evaluatePriority", () => {
  it("scores memory alignment ├Ќ2 and reports hits", () => {
    const r = evaluatePriority(brief({ what: "build the widget" }), tel({}), ["widget"]);
    expect(r.score).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);
    expect(r.reason).toContain("memory aligned");
  });

  it("avoidance adds weight and a decision-needed reason", () => {
    const r = evaluatePriority(brief({}), tel({ prompts: 6, actions: 0 }), []);
    expect(r.pattern).toBe("avoidance");
    expect(r.score).toBe(4);
    expect(r.reason).toContain("needs decision");
  });

  it("done is pushed to the bottom", () => {

    expect(r.score).toBe(-100);
    expect(r.reason).toContain("done");
  });

    const r = evaluatePriority(brief({ next: "stuck on auth" }), tel({}), []);
    expect(r.reason).toContain("explicit blocker");
  });

  it("trailing уГЅ inside a parenthetical does not count as a blocker", () => {
    const r = evaluatePriority(brief({ next: "ship A (СйютЊЂB уГЅ)" }), tel({}), []);
    expect(r.reason).not.toContain("explicit blocker");
  });

  it("concrete уГЅX does count as a blocker", () => {
    const r = evaluatePriority(brief({ next: "уГЅућеТѕитЏътцЇ" }), tel({}), []);
    expect(r.reason).toContain("explicit blocker");
  });
});
